name: Metropolix
description: Multitrack, performance-oriented pitch, gate and mod sequencer. Two tracks with 8 stages, per-stage pulse count, gate type, slide, skip, ratchet, probability and accumulator. Eight assignable MOD lanes.

controls.name: Pitch Sliders
controls.description: Set default pitch per stage. Range and root configurable per track.
controls.detail: 1–4 octave range · scale-quantized · overridable per stage

controls.name: Pulse Count
controls.description: 8-position switch sets how many clock pulses each stage holds.
controls.detail: 1–8 pulses · interacts with Gate Type and Ratchet

controls.name: Gate Type
controls.description: 4-position switch: HOLD, MULTIPLE, SINGLE, or REST.
controls.detail: HOLD=tied · MULTIPLE=gate per pulse · SINGLE=one gate · REST=silent

controls.name: CTRL 1 / CTRL 2
controls.description: Assignable performance knobs for real-time modulation of any parameter.
controls.detail: User-assignable destination · takes effect immediately

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Push-encoder for navigating and editing on-screen parameters. Turning it on HOME scrolls through 4 HOME screens.
controls.detail: Turn to scroll · press to select/confirm · long-press for edit modes

controls.name: RUN
controls.description: Start or stop sequencer playback.
controls.detail: Configurable as Run/Pause or Run/Stop in SETUP

controls.name: LOOPY (∞)
controls.description: Enters Loopy performance mode for live loop manipulation.
controls.detail: 1-finger or 2-finger loopy · Stage Player sub-mode

controls.name: RESET
controls.description: Returns sequence to starting stage. Long-press sets GATE outputs to 0V.
controls.detail: Global or per-track · responds to RESET IN jack

controls.name: SETUP
controls.description: Opens global setup menu: clock source, MIDI, presets, display settings.
controls.detail: Clock = Internal or External

controls.name: BPM
controls.description: Sets internal clock tempo.
controls.detail: Tap-tempo supported · range varies

controls.name: SCALE
controls.description: Assigns quantization scale and root note to pitch sliders.
controls.detail: 35 traditional scales + 14 chord shapes + 100 user scales (10 banks of 10)

controls.name: ORDER
controls.description: Sets playback order of stages (forward, reverse, ping-pong, random, etc.).
controls.detail: Multiple order modes · customizable shortcuts

controls.name: LEN
controls.description: Sets number of active stages in the sequence.
controls.detail: 1–8 stages

controls.name: DIV
controls.description: Sets clock division for the track relative to master clock.
controls.detail: Multiple divisions available

controls.name: SWING
controls.description: Adds swing/shuffle timing to the track.

controls.name: SLIDE
controls.description: Sets portamento (slide) time for the track.
controls.detail: Track-level global slide time · 0–100

controls.name: GATE (Track)
controls.description: Sets global gate percentage length for MULTIPLE/SINGLE gate types.
controls.detail: % of clock pulse · overridable per stage

controls.name: SLIDE (Stage)
controls.description: Enables portamento slide into the selected stage.
controls.detail: Per-stage on/off

controls.name: SKIP
controls.description: Skips the selected stage during playback.
controls.detail: Per-stage on/off

controls.name: PITCH (Stage)
controls.description: Overrides slider pitch with a specific note value for the stage.
controls.detail: Per-stage pitch override · independent of slider

controls.name: GATE (Stage)
controls.description: Overrides track gate length for the selected stage.
controls.detail: Per-stage gate percentage

controls.name: RATCH
controls.description: Adds ratcheting (note repeats) within a stage's pulse count.
controls.detail: Per-stage · up to 8 · interacts with Gate Stretching

controls.name: PROB
controls.description: Sets probability that a stage will play on any given pass.
controls.detail: Per-stage · 0–100%

controls.name: ACCUM
controls.description: Cumulative pitch transposition that grows each time stage is visited.
controls.detail: Per-stage · polarity, range, and order configurable

controls.name: CV
controls.description: Assigns a CV value or gate-toggle voltage to a stage for MOD/AUX output.
controls.detail: CV Type = CV Value or Gate Toggle

controls.name: ALT (LOAD/SAVE/A/B/CLK/MX/LIM/TYPE)
controls.description: Hold ALT then press stage button to access alternate functions.
controls.detail: LOAD/SAVE presets · A/B output assign · CLK div · MX expander · LIM accum · TYPE cv

controls.name: TRK 1 / TRK 2 / MOD
controls.description: Selects which track or MOD lane is currently being edited.
controls.detail: TRK 1 and TRK 2 are pitch/gate tracks · MOD selects one of 8 mod lanes

controls.name: EXIT
controls.description: Exits current menu and returns to HOME screen.
controls.detail: At HOME, rotate encoder to cycle through 4 HOME screens

controls.name: X / Y / Z Attenuverters
controls.description: Scale and invert the three AUX CV inputs before they modulate assigned parameters.
controls.detail: CCW = attenuated/inverted · CW = unity · center = zero

inputs.name: Clock In
inputs.description: External clock input to sync sequencer to outside source

inputs.name: Reset In
inputs.description: Trigger resets sequence to start stage (reconfigurable as RUN in SETUP)

inputs.name: X In
inputs.description: AUX CV input assignable to any sequencer parameter

inputs.name: Y In
inputs.description: AUX CV input, identical in function to X In

inputs.name: Z In
inputs.description: AUX CV input, identical in function to X In

outputs.name: A Out
outputs.description: User-assignable output A: pitch, gate, clock, or mod signal

outputs.name: B Out
outputs.description: User-assignable output B: pitch, gate, clock, or mod signal

outputs.name: Clock Out
outputs.description: Divided clock output from internal or external clock source

outputs.name: Track 1 Pitch
outputs.description: 1V/oct pitch output for Track 1 (swappable with Track 2 via Trk Out Swap)

outputs.name: Track 2 Pitch
outputs.description: 1V/oct pitch output for Track 2 (swappable with Track 1 via Trk Out Swap)

behaviors.name: Two Independent Tracks
behaviors.description: Tracks share the same 8 sliders but interpret them independently. Each has its own order, length, division, swing, slide, and gate settings. Different clock divisions create instant counterpoint.

behaviors.name: MOD Lanes
behaviors.description: Eight CV/gate lanes with their own stage values, order, length, and clock div. Assignable to any OUT jack or internal parameter. The key to chord progressions and parameter automation.

behaviors.name: Accumulator
behaviors.description: Cumulative pitch offset that grows each pulse, stage, or loop. Direction (up/down/freeze) and polarity assignable to AUX or mod lane. Limit sets ceiling before wrap or stop.

behaviors.name: Loopy Mode
behaviors.description: Two-finger: hold two stages to loop between them instantly. One-finger on Track 2: Stage Player keyboard mode — each press plays that stage's pitch once. Shift loop region while held.

behaviors.name: Ratchet Modes
behaviors.trigger: per-stage RATCH button (with Gate Stretching ON)
behaviors.description: MULTIPLY: ratchet count fits across the pulse count, so repeat rate tracks gate length. PULSE: all ratchets play within one clock pulse at a constant rate — classic ratchet. GATED: rate is fixed (one pulse per ratchet unit) but the total count stretches with gate length.

behaviors.name: Gate Types
behaviors.description: HOLD: ties gate through consecutive stages (legato). MULTI: gate per pulse (staccato). SINGLE: one gate regardless of pulse count (sustained). REST: silence, pitch still advances.

behaviors.name: Brownian Order
behaviors.description: 50% chance forward, 25% stay, 25% back. Sequence wanders but trends forward. Combined with scale quantize and accumulator limits, produces melodies that feel improvised.

behaviors.name: Track Output Swap
behaviors.trigger: Trk Out Swap via AUX, MOD lane, or CTRL
behaviors.description: Swaps Track 1 and Track 2 pitch/gate outputs instantly. Melody goes to bass voice, bass to melody. Assignable to AUX for a live crossfade surprise.

behaviors.name: Pitch Pre vs Pitch Post
behaviors.description: Pitch Pre: modulates before the quantizer so output stays in the chosen scale (good for in-scale transposition). Pitch Post: adds 1V/oct chromatic offset after the quantizer, so pitches can land outside the scale.

behaviors.name: Preset Chaining
behaviors.description: 64 presets (8 banks x 8) chainable in sequence. Chain Follow auto-advances after N loops. Use for song structures with distinct verse/chorus patterns.

patchIdeas.name: Boards of Canada chord progression
patchIdeas.patch: MOD Lane 1 assigned to Pitch Post of both tracks, /8 clock, LEN 4, stepped values transpose the whole sequence every couple of bars while sliders stay fixed.
steps: Press MOD, turn encoder to MOD Lane 1.
In the Lane Menu set DIV=/8 and LEN=4.
Press CV (stage edit). Set stage 1=0st, 2=+2st, 3=+5st, 4=+7st with the encoder.
Hold ALT then A (or B) to open the output assignment. Assign MOD Lane 1 to Pitch Post, destination Track 1+2.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO1 V/oct, Track 1 Gate → ENV1.
Patch Track 2 Pitch → VCO2 V/oct, Track 2 Gate → ENV2.
Program any 8-stage melody on the sliders, press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch and Track 2 Pitch — 1V/oct, transposed chromatically by MOD 1 every 2 bars.
patchIdeas.hear: The same sliders re-voiced over four moving roots — a slow I–ii–IV–V vibe. Change MOD stage values to remap the progression.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* MOD 1: DIV /8 · LEN 4 · values 0,+2,+5,+7 → Pitch Post
* TRK 1: running · TRK 2: running

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> ENV2 (Gate) // mod lane transposes both tracks

patchIdeas.name: Chord arpeggio within a single stage
patchIdeas.patch: One stage fires three ratchets and accumulates +4 semitones per ratchet, sounding a root–3rd–5th arpeggio in a single pulse.
steps: Pick a stage (e.g. stage 3). Set its GATE TYPE switch to SINGLE and PULSE COUNT to 1.
Press RATCH, then press that stage's button and turn encoder to 3.
Press ACCUM, press the stage, set amount to +4 semitones.
Hold ALT + LIM, set the accumulator limit to +7 semitones and Reset=Stage.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO V/oct, Track 1 Gate → ENV → VCA.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch — 1V/oct stepped through root → +4st → +7st; Track 1 Gate — three ratchet pulses.
patchIdeas.hear: A fast root–major-3rd–5th arpeggio that resolves inside the beat. Swap +4/+7 to +3/+7 for a minor triad.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* Stage: Gate=SINGLE · RATCH=3 · ACCUM +4st · LIM +7st

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // ratchets step pitch root→3rd→5th

patchIdeas.name: Two-voice counterpoint from one sequence
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 forward, Track 2 reverse and half speed, same sliders — instant two-voice counterpoint.
steps: Press TRK 1. Press ORDER and select Forward. Press DIV and set /1.
Press TRK 2. Press ORDER and select Forward, then press the encoder to flip DIRECTION to Reverse.
Press DIV on TRK 2 and set /2.
Program an 8-stage melody on the sliders.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → VCO1/ENV1.
Patch Track 2 Pitch/Gate → VCO2/ENV2.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch and Track 2 Pitch — 1V/oct melody running forward at /1 and backward at /2.
patchIdeas.hear: Two voices crossing each other from opposite ends. Shift sliders and both voices move in contrary motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: ORDER=FWD · DIV=/1
* TRK 2: ORDER=FWD DIR=REV · DIV=/2

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> ENV2 (Gate) // same sliders, opposite directions

patchIdeas.name: Generative brownian walk
patchIdeas.patch: Brownian order plus per-loop accumulator drift, with AUX controlling accumulator direction — a walking melody that keeps evolving.
steps: Press SCALE, pick a Major Pentatonic scale and a root you like.
Press TRK 1 then ORDER, select Brownian.
Press LEN, set STAGES=8.
Press ACCUM and for each stage set amount=+1st, Reset Trigger=Loop.
Hold ALT + LIM, set accumulator LIMIT to ±7 semitones, Polarity bi-directional.
Press X button (AUX X) and assign destination Accum Dir.
Patch LFO (slow) → X In, Track 1 Pitch/Gate → voice. Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch — pentatonic melody that wanders and transposes ±7st over time.
patchIdeas.hear: A drunken melody that mostly trends forward but occasionally backs up, slowly climbing then falling as the LFO flips accumulator direction.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: ORDER=BROWNIAN · LEN 8 · pentatonic
* ACCUM: +1st/loop · LIM ±7 · AUX X → Accum Dir

- LFO (Out) >> Metropolix (X In)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // wanders but drifts around root

patchIdeas.name: Live loopy performance
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 plays the full 8-stage sequence; Track 2 is in Stage Player so you tap stage buttons as a keyboard for lead lines.
steps: Program an 8-stage bass line on the sliders.
Press TRK 1 and make sure ORDER=Forward, LEN=8. This is the bass track.
Press TRK 2, press LOOPY (∞). Configure Loopy Target=Current and enable Stage Player.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → Bass VCO/ENV.
Patch Track 2 Pitch/Gate → Lead VCO/ENV.
Press RUN. Tap stage buttons to play lead notes in real time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 outputs the running bass; Track 2 Pitch/Gate fire each time you press a stage button.
patchIdeas.hear: A steady bass with a one-shot keyboard lead on top — instant performance layer without a MIDI controller.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: 8-stage sequence running
* TRK 2: LOOPY Stage Player mode

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> Bass VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> Bass ENV (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> Lead VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> Lead ENV (Gate) // Track 2 played live as keyboard

patchIdeas.name: Hemiola (3 against 4)
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 plays a straight 8. Track 2 uses PULSE COUNT=2 for three stages then rest to paint a 3+1 feel against it.
steps: TRK 1: sliders for kick pattern, every stage GATE TYPE=MULTI, PULSE COUNT=1.
TRK 2: stages 1–3 GATE TYPE=MULTI PULSE COUNT=2; stage 4 GATE TYPE=REST PULSE COUNT=2; LEN=4.
Patch Track 1 Gate → Kick Trig.
Patch Track 2 Gate → Snare Trig.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Gate — straight kick. Track 2 Gate — three hits then a rest in a 3+1 pulse grouping.
patchIdeas.hear: Snare accents fall on a 3-pulse cycle against a 4-beat kick — a rolling hemiola rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: 8 stages straight
* TRK 2: S1-3 MULTI p=2 · S4 REST p=2 · LEN=4

- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> Kick (Trig)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> Snare (Trig) // 3+rest against 4/4

patchIdeas.name: Stutter-drop fill
patchIdeas.patch: Stage 6 ratchets four times in MULTI, stage 7 rests, stage 8 resumes — a programmed stumble-and-recover every bar.
steps: TRK 1: 8 stages programmed as your melody. All stages GATE TYPE=MULTI PULSE COUNT=1.
Press RATCH, press stage 6, set to 4.
Set stage 7 GATE TYPE switch to REST.
Leave stage 8 default.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO, Track 1 Gate → ENV → VCA.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Gate — normal gates then a 4-ratchet burst on stage 6, silence on 7, single gate on 8.
patchIdeas.hear: A burst of machine-gun repeats, a dramatic drop-out, and a clean landing on stage 8 — an end-of-bar fill that feels performed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: S1-5 MULTI p=1 · S6 RATCH=4 MULTI · S7 REST · S8 default

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // burst → silence → resume

patchIdeas.name: User-scale reharmonization
patchIdeas.patch: Save several User Scales inside one bank, then use MOD Lane 1 assigned to Scale (User) to step through them — every few bars the chord center changes.
steps: Press SCALE, select User Bank 1, Scale 1-1. Long-press encoder to edit notes, set it to a chord-tone scale. Exit.
Repeat for Scales 1-2, 1-3, 1-4 with different chord tones in the same key.
Press the encoder on one scale to Lock the bank (MOD LOCK icon).
Press MOD, pick MOD Lane 1. DIV=/8, LEN=4.
Assign MOD Lane 1 destination = Scale (User). Program stages 1–4 as −1V, 0V, +1V, +2V so the lane steps one scale per change.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → voice. Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch — sliders are re-quantized into a different User Scale every /8 loop.
patchIdeas.hear: The melody recolors into new chord tones every few bars without you touching anything — instant reharmonization.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* SCALE: User Bank 1 locked · 4 scales programmed
* MOD 1: DIV /8 · LEN 4 · values -1V,0,+1V,+2V → Scale (User)
* TRK 1: running

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // scale shifts every /8 loop

patchIdeas.name: Swing offset between tracks
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 straight, Track 2 swung — same sliders, two grooves at once.
steps: Press TRK 1, press SWING, set 0%.
Press TRK 2, press SWING, set 60%.
Program your 8-stage melody on the sliders.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → VCO1/ENV1.
Patch Track 2 Pitch/Gate → VCO2/ENV2.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Both track outputs carry the same notes with different micro-timing on Track 2 Gate.
patchIdeas.hear: Two voices locked to the same notes but one shuffles — a laid-back jazz-style rhythmic layering.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: SWING=0%
* TRK 2: SWING=60%

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> ENV2 (Gate) // two grooves, same notes

patchIdeas.name: AUX as hidden panel controls
patchIdeas.patch: No cables in AUX — still use X/Y/Z attenuverters as extra performance knobs by assigning each to a parameter.
steps: Press X (AUX IN). Assign destination Accum Dir.
Press Y. Assign destination Play Order.
Press Z. Assign destination Pulses Len.
Leave the AUX jacks unpatched — the attenuverter alone provides the control voltage.
Program a sequence and press RUN.
Turn X, Y, Z attenuverters during play to re-shape the sequence live.
patchIdeas.signalOut: No external output — the three AUX knobs become three live performance controls.
patchIdeas.hear: Three extra physical knobs on the panel that morph the pattern's direction, length and accumulator without patching anything.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* AUX X (attenuverter only) → Accum Dir
* AUX Y (attenuverter only) → Play Order
* AUX Z (attenuverter only) → Pulses Len

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // knobs re-shape the sequence without cables

patchIdeas.name: Divergent dual-voice polymetric counterpoint
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 at length 7, Track 2 at length 5, same scale and clock div — phrases realign only every 35 steps.
steps: Press SCALE, pick a natural minor scale with a root you like.
Press TRK 1, press LEN, set STAGES=7.
Press TRK 2, press LEN, set STAGES=5.
Leave DIV equal on both tracks.
Program sliders so the first 7 and first 5 each make a musical phrase.
Patch Track 1 and Track 2 Pitch/Gate to two voices.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 — 7-step loop. Track 2 — 5-step loop. Both at the same clock.
patchIdeas.hear: Phrases drift in and out of phase with each other; a new alignment every 35 steps feels composed but isn't.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: LEN=7 · scale minor
* TRK 2: LEN=5 · same scale

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> ENV2 (Gate) // 7 vs 5 realigns every 35 steps

patchIdeas.name: MOD lane as filter envelope accent
patchIdeas.patch: MOD Lane 1 programmed as a per-stage CV curve into B Out, routed to filter cutoff — paints accents on specific steps without an AUX input.
steps: Press MOD, select MOD Lane 1. LEN=8.
Press CV, step through each stage and set voltages (e.g. +1V on accented stages, 0V elsewhere).
In the Lane Menu enable Slew (gives smooth motion between stages).
Hold ALT + B, assign MOD Lane 1 to B Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO, Track 1 Gate → ENV → VCA.
Patch B Out → Filter Cutoff.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: B Out — per-stage CV curve driving cutoff. Track 1 Gate — normal rhythm gates.
patchIdeas.hear: Bright opening on the accented stages and darker in between, all locked to the sequence.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* MOD 1: stepped CV per stage · slew ON → B Out
* TRK 1: pitch/gate to voice

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate)
- ENV (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Metropolix (B Out) >> VCF (Cutoff) // MOD 1 paints per-step accents

patchIdeas.name: Gate-toggle ratchet noise bursts
patchIdeas.patch: A MOD lane with CV TYPE = Gate Toggle fires only on chosen stages and drives a noise VCA — fills and hat rolls aligned to ratcheted steps of the main voice.
steps: TRK 1: program a normal pitch/gate pattern. Put RATCH=4 on stages 4 and 8.
Press MOD, pick MOD Lane 1. Hold ALT + TYPE, set CV TYPE = Gate Toggle.
Press CV and toggle stages 4 and 8 high, others low.
Hold ALT + A, assign MOD Lane 1 to A Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → main voice. Patch A Out → Noise VCA gate input.
Patch Noise source → Noise VCA input. Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A Out — gate pulses only on stages 4 and 8; Track 1 outputs the main voice normally.
patchIdeas.hear: Bursts of noise land exactly on the ratcheted beats, like snare-roll or hat fills locked to the melody.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: pitch/gate · RATCH=4 on S4,S8
* MOD 1: Gate Toggle high on S4,S8 → A Out

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (A Out) g> Noise VCA ENV (Gate)
- Noise (Out) -> Noise VCA (In) // bursts only on ratchet stages

patchIdeas.name: Self-modulating microtonal drift
patchIdeas.patch: MOD Lane 2 with tiny CV values, different length than Track 1, sums into the pitch path via a precision adder — the melody drifts microtonally.
steps: TRK 1: program your melody, LEN=8.
Press MOD, select MOD Lane 2. LEN=5.
Press CV on MOD 2, set stage values small (e.g. ±50 to ±100 mV).
Hold ALT + B, route MOD Lane 2 to B Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → Precision Adder In 1.
Patch B Out → Precision Adder In 2.
Patch Adder Out → VCO V/oct; Track 1 Gate → ENV. Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Precision Adder Out — Track 1 pitch with a slowly-cycling microtonal offset from MOD 2.
patchIdeas.hear: The same notes but slightly detuned from one pass to the next; a shimmering, alive-sounding melody that never quite repeats.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: LEN=8 · pitch melody
* MOD 2: LEN=5 · small ±50-100mV stages → B Out

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> Precision Adder (In1)
- Metropolix (B Out) p> Precision Adder (In2)
- Precision Adder (Out) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate) // microtonal drift, 8 vs 5

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic kick and bass split
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 plays a 16-step bassline, Track 2 plays a 6-step kick pattern at the same clock div — resolves every 48 steps.
steps: Press TRK 1, DIV=/1, LEN will naturally be 8; use MOD/AUX or long bass phrasing to stretch, or treat LEN=8 as a 16-pulse phrase by using MULTI stages with PULSE COUNT=2.
Press TRK 2, DIV=/1, set LEN/PULSES to deliver a 6-pulse loop (e.g. LEN=3 with PULSE COUNT=2, or LEN=6 with PULSE COUNT=1).
Slider values on TRK 1 = bass notes; slider values ignored on TRK 2 (we only use its gate).
Patch Track 1 Pitch → Bass VCO, Track 1 Gate → Bass ENV.
Patch Track 2 Gate → Kick Trig.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 — bass pitch + gate. Track 2 Gate — kick trigger every 6 pulses.
patchIdeas.hear: The kick walks a 6-pulse cycle against a 16-pulse bassline; downbeats coincide only every 48 pulses.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: DIV /1 · 16-pulse bass phrase
* TRK 2: DIV /1 · 6-pulse kick phrase

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> Bass VCO (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> Bass ENV (Gate)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) t> Kick (Trig) // 16 vs 6 → resolves at 48

patchIdeas.name: Acid slide with MOD-driven resonance sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Per-stage SLIDE for a 303 glide, MOD 1 drives filter resonance, MOD 2 drives VCA accents — all in-box programming for an acid lead.
steps: TRK 1: program pitches. Press SLIDE and toggle it on for stages 2, 5 and 7.
Press SLIDE TIME, dial in around 40.
Press MOD, MOD Lane 1. Program a rising CV ramp across 16 pulses. Route MOD 1 → A Out.
MOD Lane 2: short CV pulses on every stage (accents). Route MOD 2 → B Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO. VCO → Resonant LPF → VCA.
Track 1 Gate → ENV → VCA CV. A Out → LPF Resonance. B Out → VCA CV.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch — gliding acid line. A Out — resonance ramp. B Out — per-step VCA accents.
patchIdeas.hear: A squelchy 303 lead where resonance opens across the phrase and each step punches with its own accent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: slides on S2,S5,S7 · pitch pattern
* MOD 1: resonance ramp → A Out
* MOD 2: VCA accents → B Out

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- VCO (Out) -> LPF (In)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate)
- ENV (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Metropolix (A Out) >> LPF (Res)
- Metropolix (B Out) >> VCA (CV) // acid squelch

patchIdeas.name: Convergent chord stabs on two VCOs
patchIdeas.patch: Track 1 drives VCO1, Track 2 drives VCO2 transposed up a fifth via Pitch Post; OR their gates so either track fires a shared envelope.
steps: Press TRK 1, program the melody.
Press TRK 2, program same or different sliders.
On TRK 2 press the Track Menu → Transpose, set +7 semitones (or use MOD/AUX Pitch Post +7).
Add RATCH=2 on a couple of stages on one track for flams.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO1, Track 2 Pitch → VCO2.
Sum both Gate outputs with an OR/MAX logic module → single ENV → shared VCF/VCA.
Mix VCO1 + VCO2 into the VCF. Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch and Track 2 Pitch — two-note stab. OR Out — one fused gate.
patchIdeas.hear: Stacked fifths playing as chord stabs; flams on ratchet stages add human feel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: melody · TRK 2: Transpose +7st
* ratchets on S3,S7 for flams

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Pitch) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> OR Logic (In1)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> OR Logic (In2)
- OR Logic (Out) g> ENV (Gate)
- VCO1 (Out) -> Mixer (In1)
- VCO2 (Out) -> Mixer (In2)
- Mixer (Out) -> VCF (In)
- VCF (Out) -> VCA (In) // stacked stabs

patchIdeas.name: AUX as clocked Turing reset
patchIdeas.patch: MOD Lane 1 in Gate-Toggle mode fires only on stage 1 of a long phrase, resetting a Turing Machine so its randomness locks to the phrase boundary.
steps: Press MOD, MOD Lane 1. Hold ALT + TYPE, CV TYPE = Gate Toggle.
Press CV, toggle stage 1 high, all others low. LEN=8.
Hold ALT + A, route MOD Lane 1 to A Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch/Gate → main voice.
Patch A Out → Turing Machine Clock or Reset input.
Patch Turing CV Out → VCO2 V/oct, Track 2 Gate → ENV2.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A Out — single gate on step 1 of every phrase. Turing CV — randomness that now resets in phase with Track 1.
patchIdeas.hear: A random texture that mutates but always comes back to the same moment of the phrase — unpredictable but in time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* MOD 1: Gate Toggle S1 only → A Out
* TRK 1: main melody

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO1 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV1 (Gate)
- Metropolix (A Out) t> Turing Machine (Clock/Reset)
- Turing Machine (CV Out) p> VCO2 (V/oct)
- Metropolix (Track 2 Gate) g> ENV2 (Gate) // phrase-locked randomness

patchIdeas.name: MOD-driven wavefolder timbre evolution
patchIdeas.patch: MOD Lane 1 runs its own 5-step sequence into a wavefolder while Track 1 plays an 8-step melody — timbre and pitch slide out of phase.
steps: TRK 1: melodic pitch pattern, LEN=8.
Press MOD, MOD Lane 1. LEN=5.
Press CV, set each MOD 1 stage to a different voltage (0V … +5V).
Hold ALT + A, route MOD 1 to A Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → VCO → Wavefolder → VCA → output.
Patch A Out → Wavefolder Fold CV. Track 1 Gate → ENV → VCA.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 Pitch — melody. A Out — a 5-step fold amount CV.
patchIdeas.hear: The same pitch returns with a different timbre each time, because the fold sequence is shorter than the pitch sequence.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: LEN=8 · pitch melody
* MOD 1: LEN=5 · fold sequence → A Out

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> VCO (V/oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Wavefolder (In)
- Metropolix (A Out) >> Wavefolder (Fold)
- Wavefolder (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) g> ENV (Gate)
- ENV (Out) >> VCA (CV) // timbre decoupled from pitch

patchIdeas.name: Buzz-roll fill with pitch dive
patchIdeas.patch: Last two stages ratchet x8, slide, descend in pitch; MOD 1 shortens the decay on those stages — an end-of-phrase drum roll that pitches down.
steps: TRK 1: program 8 pitches. Raise stages 7 and 8 sliders higher than the others and tilt them downward; enable SLIDE on both stages.
Press RATCH, press stage 7 and 8, set both to 8.
Press MOD, MOD 1. Set stages 7–8 to a low CV (for short decay), others to a high CV.
Hold ALT + B, route MOD 1 to B Out.
Patch Track 1 Pitch → Drum/VCO pitch CV. Track 1 Gate → Drum trigger.
Patch B Out → ENV Decay CV.
Press RUN.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Track 1 — main rhythm with rapid ratchets on the last two stages. B Out — decay CV that dips on the roll.
patchIdeas.hear: A buzz-roll that dives pitch-wise into the loop — a classic fill without programming a separate drum machine.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Metropolix:
* TRK 1: S7-S8 RATCH=8 · slide ON · descending pitch
* MOD 1: short decay on S7-S8 → B Out

- Metropolix (Track 1 Pitch) p> Drum (Pitch CV)
- Metropolix (Track 1 Gate) t> Drum (Trig)
- Metropolix (B Out) >> ENV (Decay) // buzz-roll dive fill
